text: God told Jacob to go up to Bethel, settle there, and build an altar to the God who appeared to him fleeing Esau.
Jacob told his household to get rid of their foreign gods, purify themselves, and change their clothes.
They would go up to Bethel to build an altar to God, who answered him in his distress and had been with him.
They gave Jacob all their foreign gods and earrings, and he buried them under the oak near Shechem.
As they set out, a terror from God fell on the surrounding cities, so no one pursued them.
Jacob and his people came to Luz, that is Bethel, in Canaan.
He built an altar there and called the place El-bethel, since God had revealed himself to him fleeing his brother.
Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, died and was buried under an oak below Bethel, named Allon-bacuth.
God appeared to Jacob again after he returned from Paddan-aram and blessed him.
God said his name was no longer Jacob but Israel, and called him Israel.
God told him he was God Almighty, to be fruitful and multiply; a nation and kings would come from him.
He would give him the land promised to Abraham and Isaac and to his offspring after him.
Then God went up from him at the place where he had spoken.
Jacob set up a stone pillar where God had spoken, poured a drink offering and oil on it.
Jacob named the place Bethel.
They journeyed on from Bethel, and while still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel began to give birth and had hard labor.
During the difficult labor the midwife told her not to be afraid, for she was having another son.
As she was dying, she named him Ben-oni, but his father called him Benjamin.
Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath, that is Bethlehem.
Jacob set up a pillar over her tomb, still known as Rachel's tomb.
Israel journeyed on and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.
While Israel lived there, Reuben slept with Bilhah, his father's concubine, and Israel heard of it; Jacob had twelve sons.
Leah's sons were Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
Rachel's sons were Joseph and Benjamin.
Bilhah, Rachel's servant, bore Dan and Naphtali.
Zilpah, Leah's servant, bore Gad and Asher; these were born to Jacob in Paddan-aram.
Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, Kiriath-arba, that is Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac had lived.
Isaac lived one hundred eighty years.
Isaac breathed his last and died, old and full of days, and his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
